current_entries.en_definition: (chiefly, UK, historical) A large and heavy saucepan or frying pan with legs and, usually, a long handle designed for heating food in a fireplace.
(chiefly, US) A pan for frying, generally large and heavy.
Having the bluish-grey/gray colour of slate.
(uncountable, geology) A fine-grained homogeneous sedimentary rock composed of clay or volcanic ash which has been metamorphosed so that it cleaves easily into thin layers.
The bluish-grey colour of most slate.
A chalkboard, sheet of slate for writing on with chalk or with a thin rod of slate (a slate pencil) formerly commonly used by both students and teachers in schools.
A roofing-tile made of slate.
A record of money owed.
(transitive, chiefly, US) To schedule.
A butcher (as a profession or job).
A ritual slaughterer, kosher slaughterer, kosher butcher, shochet / shokhet.
An institution or social practice of owning human beings as property, especially for use as forced laborers.

examples.english: America did away with slavery.
examples.chinese: 美國廢除了奴隸制度。

current_entries.en_definition: A condition of servitude endured by a slave.
(figuratively) A condition in which one is captivated or subjugated, as by greed or drugs.
(footwear) A low soft shoe that can be slipped on and off easily.
(footwear) A low soft shoe intended for indoor use; a bedroom slipper or house slipper.
Of a surface, having low friction, often due to being covered in a non-viscous liquid, and therefore hard to grip, hard to stand on without falling, etc.

examples.english: Driving on a slippery road can lead to a car wreck.
examples.chinese: 在光滑的路上开车会导致车祸。

current_entries.en_definition: (figuratively, by extension) Evasive; difficult to pin down.
Any machine operated by placing a coin or token into a slot, especially:
Synonym of vending machine.
Any animal in the suborder Folivora.
(especially) Any herbivorous, arboreal South American mammal of the families Choloepodidae and Bradypodidae, noted for its slowness and inactivity.
(transitive) To make smooth.
(intransitive) To become smooth.
Disparaging or derisive in an insinuative way.
Tricky; deceptive; false; spurious; contemptible.
An instance of falling of snow.

examples.english: The train was delayed because of heavy snowfall.
examples.chinese: 火车因大雪被耽搁了。

current_entries.en_definition: The amount of snow that falls on one occasion.
A motorized vehicle that is used to push snow off flat surfaces such as roads and parking lots.
A blade-like pushing device attached to a vehicle to enable it to be used for removing snow.
(skiing, skating) A maneuver in which the tips of the skis or skates point inwards and the back ends point outwards.
The quality or state of not being intoxicated.
The state or quality of being unhurried; a state of calm.
(collective, sociology) A network of personal or business contacts, especially as facilitated by social networking on the Internet.

(internet) A service allowing users to manage and interact with their network of friends or contacts on the Internet. (Called a social network application in precise usage that reserves social network to its original sense.)
(Marxism-Leninism) The intermediate phase of social development between capitalism and communism in Marxist theory in which the state has control of the means of production.

Any of a group of later political philosophies such democratic socialism and social democracy which do not envisage the need for full state ownership of the means of production nor transition to full communism, and which are typically based on principles of community decision making, social equality and the avoidance of economic and social exclusion, with economic policy giving first preference to community goals over individual ones.
A long-standing group of people sharing cultural aspects such as language, dress, norms of behavior and artistic forms.
A group of people who meet from time to time to engage in a common interest; an association or organization.
The people of one’s country or community taken as a whole.
(chemistry) A salt of sodium hydroxide and carbonic acid, NaHCO3.
This salt used in cooking as a raising agent, as an antacid, a cleaner, etc.
Any of several forms of sexual intercourse held to be unnatural, particularly bestiality or homosexuality, but also (sometimes) anal, oral, or manual sex.
(in particular) Anal sex.
A noncommissioned soldier willing to serve any state or person who will hire them.
(figuratively, by extension) A person willing to work for or ally themself with any person or institution who will hire them.

In many common law jurisdictions, a type of lawyer whose traditional role is to offer legal services to clients apart from acting as their advocate in court. A solicitor instructs barristers to act as an advocate for their client in court, although rights of audience for solicitors vary according to jurisdiction.
In English Canada and in parts of Australia, a type of lawyer who historically held the same role as above, but whose role has in modern times been merged with that of a barrister.
A bond of unity or agreement between individuals, united around a common goal or against a common enemy, such as the unifying principle that defines the labor movement; mutual support within a group.
Willingness to support another person in a difficult position or needs affection.
A canton of Switzerland.
The capital city of Solothurn canton, Switzerland.
An ethnically Somali geographic region of East Africa comprising Somalia, Djibouti, and parts of Ethiopia.

A geographic region and de facto independent unrecognized state in East Africa, internationally recognized as part of Somalia; formerly the Somali Coast Protectorate. Official name: Republic of Somaliland.
Dark; gloomy; shadowy, dimly lit.
Dull or dark in colour or brightness.
Melancholic, gloomy, dreary, dismal; grim.
Grave; extremely serious.
One's male offspring.

examples.english: Your son took part in the student movement, I hear.
examples.chinese: 聽說你兒子參與了學生運動。

current_entries.en_definition: A male person who has such a close relationship with an older or otherwise more authoritative person that he can be regarded as a son of the other person.
A familiar address to a male person from an older or otherwise more authoritative person.
A male adopted person in relation to his adoptive parents.
(transitive) To restore to ease, comfort, or tranquility; relieve; calm; quiet; refresh.
(transitive) To calm or placate someone or some situation.
(transitive) To ease or relieve pain or suffering.
A general type.

examples.english: I sort of understand.
examples.chinese: 我大概懂了。

current_entries.en_definition: (mathematics) A type.
(transitive) To separate items into different categories according to certain criteria that determine their sorts.
(religion, folklore) The spirit or essence of a person usually thought to consist of one's thoughts and personality, often believed to live on after the person's death.

examples.english: There wasn't a soul in sight.
examples.chinese: 一個人影也看不到。

current_entries.en_definition: Life, energy, vigor.
A person, especially as one among many.
Species of Prunus subg. Cerasus, (cherries), native to much of Europe and southwest Asia, especially, Prunus cerasus, Prunus pseudocerasus, and Prunus cerasoides.
The fruit of these trees, often used for cooking.
A rural locality in Charters Towers Region, northern Queensland.
A town in the Shire of Yilgarn, Western Australia.
(US) A resident of the American South, often specifically a white resident of the American South.
A native or inhabitant of the south of a region (or of the world as a whole), such as the United Kingdom.
(historical) A former transcontinental country in Europe and Asia (1922–1991), now split into Russia and 14 other countries. Official name: Union of Soviet Socialist Republics.
(informal, historical, synecdoche) The Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic.
A common East Asian liquid sauce, made by subjecting boiled beans to long fermentation and then long digestion in salt and water.
(uncountable, often, attributive) Soybeans, or the protein derived from them.
(uncountable, physics) The four-dimensional continuum of the three spatial dimensions plus time.

(physics) An n-dimensional continuum consisting of dimensions of both space and time. Normally spacetime is considered as having 4 dimensions (x, y, z, t), but higher-dimensional spacetimes are often encountered in theoretical physics, e.g. the 5-dimensional spacetime of Kaluza-Klein theory or the 11 dimensions of spacetime in M-theory.
A (sudden) flood or inundation of water; specifically, a flood in or overflow of a river or other watercourse due to heavy rain or melting snow; (uncountable, archaic) flooding, inundation.
(countable, figurative) A sudden increase or rush of something; a flood, an outburst, an outpouring.
Pertaining to (the dimension of) space.
(uncommon) Pertaining to (outer) space.
The numerous eggs of an aquatic organism.
Mushroom mycelium prepared for (aided) propagation.
(transitive) To generate, bring into being, especially non-mammalian beings in very large numbers.
(transitive) To bring forth in general.
(ergative, video games) To appear, or cause (something or someone) to appear, spontaneously in a game world at a particular place and time.
(politics) .

examples.english: Being a good conversationalist does not just mean being a good speaker of English.
examples.chinese: 作為一個良好的交談者，並不只意味著作一個英語說得好的說話者。

current_entries.en_definition: Loudspeaker.
(politics) The chair or presiding officer of certain legislative bodies, such as the U.K. House of Commons or the U.S. House of Representatives.
An explicit set of requirements to be satisfied by a material, product, or service.
An act of specifying.
An individual instance that represents a class; an example.
A sample, especially one used for diagnostic analysis.
A range; a continuous, infinite, one-dimensional set, possibly bounded by extremes.

Specifically, a range of colours representing light (electromagnetic radiation) of contiguous frequencies; hence electromagnetic spectrum, visible spectrum, ultraviolet spectrum, etc. [from later 17th c.]
(mathematics, linear algebra) The set of eigenvalues of a matrix.

(mathematics, functional analysis) Of a bounded linear operator A, the set of scalar values λ such that the operator A—λI, where I denotes the identity operator, does not have a bounded inverse; intended as a generalisation of the linear algebra sense.

(commutative algebra, algebraic geometry) An abstract object in mathematics created from a commutative ring R and denoted \operatorname{Spec}(R) or \operatorname{Spec} R and said to be the spectrum of R; useful in the study of such rings for providing a geometric object which encodes many of the properties R, and in modern geometry for generalizing the notion of an algebraic variety to that of an affine scheme. Formally, the set of all prime ideals R equipped with the Zariski topology and augmented with a sheaf of rings called the structure sheaf, generated by the B-sheaf on the distinguished open sets D_f which assigns the localization of R at f to each set D_f, regarded as a ring of functions on D_f. See w:Spectrum of a ring on Wikipedia.Wikipedia
The act, practice, ability, or subject of forming words with letters, or of reading the letters of words; orthography.

examples.english: I can't find any logic to English spelling. “Ghoti” can be pronounced as “fish.”
examples.chinese: 我找不到任何英文拼寫的邏輯。"Ghoti" 可以被發音成"fish"。

current_entries.en_definition: The manner of spelling of words; correct spelling.
A specific spelling of a word.
The reproductive cell or gamete of the male; a spermatozoon.
(uncountable, slang) Semen; the generative substance of male animals.
An object which appears to be bounded by a sphere; a round object, a ball. [from 14th c.]

examples.english: The subject is out of his sphere.
examples.chinese: 這個主題超出了他所能接受的範圍。

current_entries.en_definition: (figuratively) The region in which something or someone is active; one's province, domain. [from 17th c.]
(logic, dated) The domain of reference of a proposition, subject, or predicate, or the totality of the particular subjects to which it applies.
(mythology) A creature with the head of a person and the body of an animal, commonly a lion.
(figurative) A person who keeps their thoughts and intentions secret; an enigmatic or impassive person.
A very tall, narrow heel on a shoe.
A shoe with such a heel.
The state or quality of being spiteful.
The result or product of being spiteful.

(anatomy, immunology) In vertebrates, including humans, a ductless vascular gland, located in the left upper abdomen near the stomach, which destroys old red blood cells, removes debris from the bloodstream, acts as a reservoir of blood, and produces lymphocytes.
(archaic, except in the set phrase "to vent one's spleen") A bad mood; spitefulness. Compare gall.
(obsolete, rare) A sudden motion or action; a fit; a freak; a whim.
(obsolete) Melancholy; hypochondriacal affections.
A fit of immoderate laughter or merriment.
Self-generated; happening without any apparent external cause.
Done by one's own free choice, or without planning.
A board consisting of a flexible, springy, cantilevered platform that propels one into the air, used for diving or gymnastics.
(figuratively) Something that acts as a catalyst to begin or accelerate an action or growth.
(mechanical engineering) A toothed wheel that enmeshes with a chain or other perforated band.
(usually, in the plural) The tooth of such a wheel.
A new growth of or on a plant, whether from seed or other parts.
(figurative) A child.
(gardening, intransitive) To grow from seed; to germinate.
(military, historical, army) A body of cavalry comprising two companies or troops, averaging from one hundred and twenty to two hundred soldiers.
(military, navy) A detachment of vessels employed on any particular service or station, under the command of the senior officer
(military, air force) A tactical air force unit; consists of at least two flights; multiple squadrons make up a group or wing (depending on particular air force).
Any of several carnivorous marine cephalopod mollusks, of the order Teuthida, having a mantle, eight arms, and a pair of tentacles
A fishhook with a piece of bright lead, bone, or other substance fastened on its shank to imitate a squid.
(US, military, slang, mildly, pejorative) A sailor in the Navy.
(UK, slang, humorous, rare) A quid; one pound sterling.
(inexact) A vampire squid.
Any of numerous large beetles of the family Lucanidae, the males having elaborate mandibles resembling the antlers of a stag
especially, the largest beetle in the UK, of species Lucanus cervus.
A male horse kept primarily as a stud.

examples.english: Is this a stallion or a mare?
examples.chinese: 那是一匹公馬還是母馬？

current_entries.en_definition: An adult male horse.
(sometimes, figurative) The place where a journey starts.
The first steps when commencing an activity.
(derogatory) One who engages in covert surveillence or similar activities associated with secret police.
(derogatory) One who imposes one's views on others; one who is unfairly oppressive or needlessly strict.
A declaration or remark.

examples.english: I would like to retract my previous statement.
examples.chinese: 我想撤回我之前的陈述。

current_entries.en_definition: A presentation of opinion or position.
(computing) An instruction in a computer program, especially one that returns no value, as opposed to a function call.
An electric charge that has built up on an insulated body, often due to friction.
The electric discharge from such a body.
A discipline, principally within applied mathematics, concerned with the systematic study of the collection, presentation, analysis, and interpretation of data.

examples.english: The aviation expert analyzed the statistics in detail.
examples.chinese: 航空专家详细分析了统计数据。

current_entries.en_definition: A systematic collection of data on measurements or observations, often related to demographic information such as population counts, incomes, population counts at different ages, etc.
The suspended condensate (cloud) formed by water vapour when it encounters colder air.

examples.english: It's very important to know how to let off steam, living in this world.
examples.chinese: 生活在這個世界裏，懂得發泄是十分重要的。

current_entries.en_definition: Pressurized water vapour used for heating, cooking, or to provide mechanical energy.
(figuratively) Internal energy for progress or motive power.
(figuratively) Pent-up anger.
(transitive, cooking) To cook with steam.
(biblical character) The first Christian martyr.

examples.english: Stephen Colbert is a pretty crazy guy.
examples.chinese: 史蒂芬·科拜尔是一个好疯狂的家伙。

current_entries.en_definition: A en given name.
The grasslands of Eastern Europe and Asia. [from 1671]
A vast, cold, dry, grassy plain.
Of sound, music, etc, recorded in stereo.

examples.english: Put a record on the stereo.
examples.chinese: 往立体声音响上放一枚唱片。

current_entries.en_definition: (by extension) Any object or device equipped with audio components that reproduces sound in stereo, such as a stereo console in the home.
(printing, colloquial) A stereotype.
(uncomparable) Unable to reproduce (or procreate).
Germless; free from all living or viable microorganisms.
(transitive) To make unable to produce; to make unprofitable.

(transitive, biology) To kill, deactivate (denature), or destroy (break apart) all living, viable microorganisms and spores on a surface, in a fluid, or contained in a compound, such as culture media or a medical product.
Something or someone that sticks (pierces, or adheres).
An adhesive label or decal.
A wooden strip placed between courses of lumber to allow air circulation and to create a gap so the unit can be picked up with a forklift (also kiln sticker).
Tending to stick; able to adhere via the drying of a viscous substance.
(finance) Tending to stay the same; resistant to change.
(Internet, of a website) Compelling enough to keep visitors from leaving.
Rigidity or a measure of rigidity.
Inflexibility or a measure of inflexibility.
A mark of infamy or disgrace.
A scar or birthmark.
(Christianity, chiefly, _, in the plural, _, stigmata) A mark on the body corresponding to one of the wounds of the Crucifixion on Jesus's body, and sometimes reported to bleed periodically.
An external phenomenon that has an influence on a system, by triggering or modifying an internal phenomenon; for example, a spur or incentive that drives a person to take action or change behaviour.
(physiology, psychology, medicine) Something external that elicits or influences a physiological or psychological activity or response, or that affects any of the sensory apparatuses.
A building that trades stocks in of companies for money and vice versa.
An organisation that trades stocks in of companies for money and vice versa.

(meteorology) A disturbed state of the atmosphere between a severe or strong gale and a hurricane on the modern Beaufort scale, with a wind speed of between 89 and 102 kilometres per hour (55–63 miles per hour; 10 on the scale, known as a "storm" or whole gale), or of between 103 and 117 kilometres per hour (64–72 miles per hour; 11 on the scale, known as a "violent storm").

examples.english: After a storm comes a calm.
examples.chinese: 暴風過後是寧靜。

current_entries.en_definition: Any disturbed state of the atmosphere causing destructive or unpleasant weather, especially one affecting the earth's surface involving strong winds (leading to high waves at sea) and usually lightning, thunder, and precipitation.
(military) A violent assault on a fortified position or stronghold.
(chiefly, military) To violently assault (a fortified position or stronghold, a building, etc.) with the aim of gaining control of it.
Not normal; odd, unusual, surprising, out of the ordinary, often with a negative connotation.

examples.english: I saw something strange in the sky.
examples.chinese: 我看见天上有奇怪的东西。

current_entries.en_definition: (particle physics) Having the quantum mechanical property of strangeness.
Unfamiliar, not yet part of one's experience.
(obsolete) Belonging to another country; foreign.
The product or result of being strange.
(particle physics) One of the quantum numbers of subatomic particles, depending upon the relative number of strange quarks and anti-strange quarks.
(transitive) To kill someone by squeezing the throat so as to cut off the oxygen supply; to choke, suffocate or throttle.
(intransitive) To be killed by strangulation, or become strangled.
The science and art of military command as applied to the overall planning and conduct of warfare.

examples.english: Hope is not a strategy.
examples.chinese: 希望不是一种策略。

current_entries.en_definition: A plan of action intended to accomplish a specific goal.
The act of strategizing; the development of effective strategies.
(transitive) To make strong or stronger; to add strength to; to increase the strength of; to fortify.

examples.english: It will also help to strengthen the city's economy.
examples.chinese: 它也会促进城市的经济发展。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To empower; to give moral strength to; to encourage; to enhearten.
Exact; accurate; precise; rigorously particular.
Governed or governing by exact rules; observing exact rules; severe; rigorous.
Rigidly interpreted; exactly limited; confined; restricted.
A village in Dobrianka settlement hromada, Chernihiv Raion, Chernihiv Oblast, Ukraine, first mentioned in 1776.
A village in Dvorichna settlement hromada, Kupiansk Raion, Kharkiv Oblast, Ukraine, founded in 1899.
Unable to move.

examples.english: I got a fish bone stuck in my throat.
examples.chinese: 我被鱼骨刺到了喉咙。

current_entries.en_definition: Unable to progress with a task.
An artist’s or photographer’s workshop or the room in which an artist works.
A place where radio or television programs, records or films are made.
(informal) Perplexed, confused.
(cricket, of a batsman, not comparable) out as a result of the wicket-keeper breaking the wicket with the ball while the batsman is out of his crease.
(slang, archaic) Done for; bankrupt or ruined.
The property of being stupid.

examples.english: You should be ashamed of your stupidity.
examples.chinese: 你應該為你的愚蠢感到羞恥。

current_entries.en_definition: An act that is stupid.
(writing) An ancient writing implement consisting of a small rod with a pointed end for scratching letters on clay, wax-covered tablets or other surfaces, and a blunt end for obliterating them.
A hard-pointed pen-shaped instrument for marking on stencils used in a mimeograph machine.
A hard point, typically of diamond or sapphire, following a groove in a phonograph and transmitting the recorded sound for reproduction.
(Greek mythology) The river, in Hades, over which the souls of the dead are ferried by Charon.
(astronomy) The 5th moon of Pluto, discovered in 2012.
The act of submitting or yielding; surrender.

examples.english: We have the alternative of death and submission.
examples.chinese: 我們要選擇是死亡還是臣服。

current_entries.en_definition: The act of submitting or giving e.g. a completed piece of work.
(intransitive) To yield or give way to another.
(ambitransitive) To enter or put forward for approval, consideration, marking etc.
(intransitive) To sink or fall to the bottom; to settle, as lees.
(intransitive) To fall downward; to become lower; to descend; to sink.
Physical matter; material.

examples.english: The substance is light enough to float on the water.
examples.chinese: 這種物質很輕，可以浮在水面上。

current_entries.en_definition: The essential part of anything; the most vital part.
(arithmetic, uncountable) The process of subtracting a number from another.
(arithmetic, countable) A calculation involving subtracting.
The act of overthrowing a government or a ruler; dethronement.
A systematic attempt to overthrow a government by working from within; undermining.
(North America, Scotland, South Africa) A train that runs on such an underground railway.

examples.english: Where do I get the subway?
examples.chinese: 我在哪裡坐地鐵？

current_entries.en_definition: (British) An underground walkway, tunnel for pedestrians (called pedestrian underpass in US).
(Canada, US) A rapid transit system, regardless of the elevation of its right of way; a metro system.
A passing of royal powers.
A sequence of things in order.
An act of following in sequence.
Instantaneous, unexpected death not caused by violence or trauma.
(sports, games) A period or mode of play in which the next player or team to score wins instantly; often in an extra period of time following a tie at the end of the regular period of play.
A city in northeastern Egypt.

examples.english: The ship sailed through the Suez Canal.
examples.chinese: 船開過蘇伊士運河。

current_entries.en_definition: A governorate of Egypt around the city and canal.
(ergative) To suffer, or cause someone to suffer, from severely reduced oxygen intake to the body.
(ergative) To die due to, or kill someone by means of, insufficient oxygen supply to the body.

(card games) Each of the sets of a pack of cards distinguished by colour and/or specific emblems, such as the spades, hearts, diamonds, or clubs of traditional Anglo, Hispanic, and French playing cards.

examples.english: Your shoes do not go with the suit.
examples.chinese: 你的鞋子和衣服不合襯。

current_entries.en_definition: (obsolete) Regular order; succession.
(transitive, said of clothes, hairstyle or other fashion item) To be suitable or apt for one's image.
(intransitive, transitive) To please; to make content; to fit someone's (or one's own) taste.
(intransitive) To agree; to be fitted; to correspond (usually followed by to, archaically also followed by with).
A group or train of attendants, servants etc.; a retinue. [from 16th c.]
A connected series or succession of objects; a number of things used or classed together. [from 16th c.]
A group of connected rooms, usually separable from other rooms by means of access. [from 18th c.]

(music) An excerpt of instrumental music from a larger work that contains other elements besides the music; for example, the Nutcracker Suite is the music (but not the dancing) from the ballet The Nutcracker, and the Carmen Suite is the instrumental music (but not the singing and dancing) from the opera Carmen.
A village in Valky urban hromada, Bohodukhiv Raion, Kharkiv Oblast, Ukraine, founded in 1917.
A village in Mostove rural hromada, Voznesensk Raion, Mykolaiv Oblast, Ukraine, founded in 1919.
A village in Veselynove settlement hromada, Voznesensk Raion, Mykolaiv Oblast, Ukraine, founded in 1896.
The direct rays, light or warmth of the sun.
A location on which the sun's rays fall.
(figurative) Geniality or cheerfulness.
A source of cheerfulness or joy.
(anatomy, relational, often with to) Closer to the surface of the body; especially, situated or occurring on the skin or immediately beneath it.

examples.english: He has only a superficial knowledge of Japanese.
examples.chinese: 他對日文只是略懂皮毛。

current_entries.en_definition: (relational) Existing, occurring, or located on the surface.
​Lacking depth of character or understanding; lacking substance or significance.
A fictional comic-book superhero with superhuman strength and speed, X-ray vision, and the ability to fly. [from 1933.]
(chiefly, philosophy) An imagined superior type of human being representing a new stage of human development; an übermensch, an overman. [from 1903.]
synonyms: lucy in the sky with diamonds

current_entries.en_definition: A man of extraordinary or seemingly superhuman powers.
synonyms: lucy in the sky with diamonds

current_entries.en_definition: Food consumed before going to bed.

examples.english: You should apologize to Dad for not coming home in time for supper.
examples.chinese: 你應該為你沒有及時回家吃晚餐向你父親道歉。

current_entries.en_definition: Any meal eaten in the evening; dinner eaten in the evening, rather than at noon.
(intransitive) To eat dinner.
(transitive, intransitive) To take for granted; to conclude, with less than absolute supporting data; to believe.

examples.english: I suppose it's different when you think about it over the long term.
examples.chinese: 我认为你考虑久了就会不一样了。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To theorize or hypothesize.
(medicine, usually, _, uncountable) A procedure involving major incisions to remove, repair, or replace a part of a body.

examples.english: Surgery is the best solution.
examples.chinese: 手術是最佳解決方案。

current_entries.en_definition: (medicine) The medical specialty related to the performance of surgical procedures.
Something unexpected.

examples.english: It doesn't surprise me.
examples.chinese: 这并不让我惊讶。

current_entries.en_definition: The feeling that something unexpected has happened.
(transitive) To cause (someone) to feel unusually alarmed or delighted by something unexpected.
The unpleasant emotion of anxiety or apprehension in an uncertain situation.
(legal) A temporary cessation of one's right; suspension, as when the rent or other profits of land cease by unity of possession of land and rent.

(ecology) A means of configuring civilization and human activity so that society, its members and its economies are able to meet their needs and express their greatest potential in the present, while preserving biodiversity and natural ecosystems, planning and acting for the ability to maintain these necessary resources for future generations.
The ability to sustain something.
(international, _, politics) A dominant nation or state that has control over the international affairs of a subservient state which otherwise has domestic autonomy.
(historical) A feudal landowner to whom vassals were forced to pledge allegiance.
(informal) Used to indicate that the speaker is swearing to God.
(informal) Used to indicate a high level of seriousness.
A knitted jacket or jersey, usually of thick wool, worn by athletes before or after exercise.

examples.english: You're wearing your sweater inside out.
examples.chinese: 你毛衣穿反了哟。

current_entries.en_definition: (chiefly US) A similar garment worn for warmth.
(curling) A person who sweeps the ice ahead of the rock in play.
(cricket) A fielding position along the boundary; a fielder in this position.

(transitive) To cause to appear; to find by searching, etc.
Having a pale greenish-blue colour.
A sky-blue, greenish-blue, or greenish-gray semi-precious gemstone.
(countable, and, uncountable) A pale greenish-blue colour, like that of the gemstone.
The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or solid.

examples.english: A nail penetrated the car tyre.
examples.chinese: 車胎被釘子刺穿了。

current_entries.en_definition: The metal#Adjective rim, or metal covering on a rim, of a (wooden or metal) wheel, usually of steel or formerly wrought iron, as found on (horse-drawn or railway) carriages and wagons and on locomotives.
Being everywhere at once: omnipresent.
Appearing to be everywhere at once; being or seeming to be in more than one location at the same time.
Expression of thought, confusion, or uncertainty.
Space filler or pause during conversation.
An authoritative proclamation; an edict, especially decreed by a Russian czar or later ruler.
(figuratively) Any absolutist order or arrogant proclamation
Of a brilliant dark blue or slightly purplish color like that of the pigment (noun sense 1).
In full ultramarine blue: a brilliant blue pigment traditionally made from ground-up lapis lazuli, and now usually either extracted from mineral deposits or made synthetically.
A brilliant dark blue or slightly purplish colour like that of the pigment.
Defenceless and lacking weapons.
Lacking both weapons and protective armour.
Not yet born; yet to come; future.
Not yet delivered; still existing in the mother's womb.
Existing without birth or beginning.
So many as to be incapable of being counted.
(mathematics) Incapable of being put into one-to-one correspondence with the natural numbers or any subset thereof.
(grammar, of a noun) That cannot be used freely with numbers or the indefinite article, and therefore usually takes no plural form. Example: information.
(idiomatic) ill or gloomy, especially from a cold or flu.
(idiomatic) Somewhat intoxicated or suffering from a hangover.
Below the legal age (usually age 18) for some activity, such as consuming alcohol or engaging in sexual intercourse.

examples.english: Underage drinking is a crime.
examples.chinese: 未满年龄饮酒是罪行。

current_entries.en_definition: Not yet a legal adult; still a minor.
Performed or happening in secret.
Employed or engaged in spying or secret investigation.
A person who works undercover.
(transitive) To experience; to pass through a phase.
(transitive) To suffer or endure; bear with.
